title: Coleus otostegioides
taxonomy: Coleus otostegioides is classified under the family Lamiaceae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants. Coleus otostegioides is further divided into the genera Solenostemon and Plectranthus in some classifications. However, the classification of Coleus otostegioides can be complex and may vary depending on the source. It is often considered a synonym for Solenostemon scutellarioides by some taxonomists.

intro: Coleus otostegioides, also known as Blue Pimpernel or Coleus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical Africa and Madagascar, characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Coleus otostegioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Coleus otostegioides is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
